criteriaCoverage.evidence: hard-bounce-complaint.integration (real PG): Permanent bounce -> suppressions hard_bounce + global_hard_bounces + profiles bounced + email_events; dispatcher buildIsSuppressedQuery true. Complaint -> suppression complaint + email_status complained + NO global row.

suppression-scoping.integration (wsA/wsB same email): A's complaint -> no (B,email) row, query(B)=false. Hard bounce in A -> per-workspace row only in A but query TRUE in BOTH via global_hard_bounces arm; B still no per-workspace row. Both halves asserted.

auto-suspend-isolation.integration: offender 200 sends/~20 bounces (10%) -> suspended; healthy 200/1 stays active. Rate per-workspace (buildReputationRateQuery scopes numerator+denominator by workspace_id $1). MIN_SENT_FOR_RATE=50 guard unit-verified. Suspend WHERE id=$1.

idempotency.integration: same ses_message_id 3x -> 1 email_events, 1 suppression, 1 global row via UNIQUE index (workspace_id,ses_message_id,type); raw dup raises 23505. soft-bounce-after-n: suppress only at Nth DISTINCT (count DISTINCT ses_message_id); replay doesn't advance.

recommendations: Idempotency UNIQUE index includes nullable ses_message_id (NULLs distinct in PG); not a practical gap since SES bounce/complaint always carry mail.messageId, but consider NOT NULL/partial key if non-feedback paths insert NULL ids.
Add a comment that tag workspace_id is sender-set never client-influenced inbound (audit hardening).
Add an integration test seeding a tiny denominator + high bounce ratio asserting no suspend against real PG (MIN_SENT_FOR_RATE end-to-end).
